meaning: adj.严峻的,严厉的,操行上一丝不苟的,简朴的
examples: The life of the troops was still comparatively austere.
The room was austere, nearly barren of furniture or decoration.
These days the word "kefir" is consequently more likely to bring to mind glamorous, yoga mat-toting women from Los Angeles than austere visions of blustery Eastern Europe.
The country's austere Protestantism helps to explain this, but so may a culture of industrial might.
Vale of Glamorgan MP Alun Cairns said a 7% increase "during these austere times is disappointing".
